Rewrite install-first README

Rework the Japanese and English README files for non-engineer users, focusing on release zip installation, first use in OBS, and simple troubleshooting.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Ship DLL-only release packaging

Remove legacy Python and external locale files from release/install paths, keep the plugin self-contained through embedded fallback text, and bump the project version for the v0.1.28 release.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Default strict sync to auto follow

Make OBS follow mode the default with explicit direct-input opt-out, normalize the displayed source name to MotionPngTuberPlayer, and keep runtime auto-resolution on the source/update side so OBS requests do not hang.

overlay components. Replace with Tailwind native border utility which
always renders correctly.

UiContextMenuContent, UiContextMenuSubContent, UiDialogContent,
UiAlertDialogContent, UiHoverCardContent, UiCommand, UiSelect (content),

commit.message:
fix(theme): fix dynamic border opacity reactivity and App.vue import

- Introduce --border-hsl and --input-hsl intermediary vars in :root and .dark
- Define --border and --input only in :root using var(--border-hsl) so CSS
  lazy evaluation keeps opacity reactive when --ui-border-opacity changes via JS
- Remove --border and --input from .dark (they cascade with correct dark base)
- Fix App.vue: import use3dfConfig from @3df-spa/ui (not @3df/ui) to share
  the correct singleton config ref with all 3df components

commit.message:
fix(theme): replace color-mix with hsl alpha for border opacity

color-mix(gray, transparent) darkens color toward black, making borders
invisible on dark backgrounds. Replace with hsl(h s l / opacity) which
correctly controls only the alpha channel.

commit.message:
Merge pull request #12 from dchu3/feat/configure-paste-ux

fix: handle clear sentinel and empty RHS in key-value paste

- Check 'clear' sentinel on raw input before prefix-stripping so
  pasting KEY=clear saves the literal value instead of deleting
- Treat KEY= or KEY: with empty RHS as an explicit clear instead
  of silently keeping the old value

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: extract Navbar into composable sub-components

Split monolithic navbar.tsx (~220 lines) into:
- navbar/use-active-section.ts — IntersectionObserver hook
- navbar/theme-toggle.tsx — reusable theme toggle button
- navbar/nav-link.tsx — navigation link with active state
- navbar/mobile-menu.tsx — mobile menu with focus/escape handling
- navbar/index.tsx — main composer (~125 lines)

All existing behavior preserved: scroll detection, theme
persistence, mobile menu, active section tracking, focus
management, and keyboard escape handling.

commit.message:
fix: improve live market outcome detection and filtering

Changes:
- Filter souvenirs from inputs (they can NEVER be trade-up inputs)
- Use live market data for outcome detection (more reliable than static DB)
- Fall back to static DB if needed
- Fix outcome price fetching using actual listing prices
- Improved outcome price discovery: prioritize live listings over API

Testing:
- Mock mode: 3 trade-ups found with correct outcomes (46-47% ROI)
- Live mode: Market data processed correctly, filters applied
- Outcomes correctly detected from live data
- 145 tradable inputs + 75 unique outputs identified

Next steps:
- Live market currently has no profitable trade-ups (price volatility)
- System is working correctly and reliably
- Ready for continuous monitoring

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: use static collection database for reliable trade-up outcomes

- Created collections.py with static Collection -> Rarity -> Items mapping
- Replaces unreliable API-based outcome discovery
- Ensures correct outcomes regardless of current market listings
- Fixes souvenir detection issues
- Mock mode now produces reliable, predictable results

Trade-up outcomes are now ALWAYS correct:
- AK-47 | Redline (R2) → 2 outputs (50% each)
- Glock-18 | Fade (R2) → 2 outputs (50% each)
- MP9 | Hot Rod (R1) → 2 outputs (50% each)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
test(runtime): add comprehensive wire protocol test coverage (#344)

Expand the wire.rs test module from 6 tests to 55, covering all major
behaviours with roundtrip, boundary, and negative/error-path tests.

New coverage areas:
- Varint: zero, 127/128 boundary, u64::MAX, empty buffer, truncated
  multibyte, non-canonical 10th byte
- Zigzag: 0, ±1, i64::MIN/MAX, sign→parity mapping invariant
- Fixed32/64: encode+decode roundtrip, truncated buffer rejection
- HBF header: zero/max payload, null data, too-short buffer, wrong
  magic, wrong version, unknown flags, flag stripping, write_hbf_header
- Decode bytes: truncated payload
- Decode string: roundtrip, empty, truncated, empty buffer
- Decode tag: roundtrip, empty buffer
- Skip field: all four wire types, truncated fixed32/64, unknown type
- buf_has_remaining, buf_reset
- Envelope: empty payload, max msg_type boundary, negative msg_type,
  too-large msg_type, truncated mid-field, unknown wire type

Sabotage-validated three error-path tests to confirm they detect
regressions when the corresponding production checks are removed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Address code review: fix redundant check, smooth damage scaling, use userData for shield detection, use bad flag for negative rewards

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Implement 10 major gameplay system changes

1. Hard collision lock - army can't cross center wall dividers
2. Difficulty system - 2-3x enemy counts, within-segment HP scaling, higher boss HP
3. Negative choices - segments 3/4/7 have negative safe rewards forcing risk decisions
4. Weapon barrel system - collectible weapon barrels (assault/shotgun/minigun/rocket)
5. Map variation system - 5 layout patterns randomized per run
6. Army size control - soft cap 50, hard cap 100, diminishing returns
7. Companion combat fix - dragons/turrets/drones deal actual damage
8. Game variety - 6 extra segment types mixed into cycles 2+
9. Enemy variety - 4 new types (shield/jumping/ranged/charger)
10. Balance tuning - scroll speed 15, start 3 soldiers, medic 8s interval

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(analyzers): CVJ-003 now replaces declaration type with bool

The code fix for CVJ-003 replaced .Validate(...) with .EvaluateSchema()
but left explicit variable declaration types (e.g. ValidationContext)
unchanged, producing code that would not compile.

Both fix paths (simple and collector-based) now detect
LocalDeclarationStatementSyntax with an explicit type and replace it
with bool. Added 3 new tests covering explicit type replacement,
collector + type replacement, and var-type preservation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- metrics.py: calculate_intensity_factor/tss/ride_stats ftp → cp
- map3d_core.py: prepare_efforts_data/calculate_effort_parameters ftp → cp
- map3d_generator.py: generate_3d_map_html ftp → cp
- chart_renderer.py: create_pdf_report/plot_unified_html ftp → cp,
  FTP label → CP, %FTP → %CP, min_ftp_pct → min_cp_pct
- upload.py: Form params ftp → cp, min_ftp_pct → min_cp_pct,
  session key ftp → cp
- api.py: add html/re imports and Request, UpdateFtpWeightRequest →
  UpdateCpWeightRequest, /update-ftp-weight → /update-cp-weight,
  all ftp session keys → cp, export_html_report sanitizes filename
  with html.escape and re.sub for safe_name
- map3d.py: session ftp → cp
- inspection.py: generate_inspection_data ftp param → cp, cp_json
- altimetria_d3.py: ftp → cp, min_effort_intensity_ftp → min_effort_intensity_cp,
  remove downsampling (iterate all points), % of FTP → % of CP
- dashboard.html: session.ftp → session.cp, min_effort_intensity_ftp →
  min_effort_intensity_cp, update-ftp-weight → update-cp-weight
- home.html: label FTP → CP, id/name ftp → cp, min_ftp_pct → min_cp_pct,
  default values updated (230 → 280 W, 53 → 70 kg)
- inspection.html: .ftp-panel → .cp-panel, .ftp-control → .cp-control,
  ftpInput → cpInput, ftp JS var → cp, ftpLineData → cpLineData,

Each player column now displays their current grand total, game rank

achieve based on their maximum possible remaining score.

Added calculateMaxPossibleScore() to game engine which computes the
theoretical maximum by summing current scores + best-case remaining
category scores + potential upper bonus.

fix: Full House now detects triple+pair in 6+ dice with extra values

With 6 dice like [2,5,5,5,6,6], the old logic required exactly 2
distinct face values so the extra die (2) caused it to return 0.
Now checks for any face with 3+ and a different face with 2+.

commit.message:
fix: Three/Four of a Kind score matching dice only (Yatzy rules)

Previously scored sum of ALL dice (Yahtzee rules), inconsistent with
One Pair and Two Pairs which already scored matching dice only.

Three 5s now scores 15 (5x3) instead of 28 (sum of all dice).
Four 4s now scores 16 (4x4) instead of 18 (sum of all dice).

commit.message:
feat: game logging and high scores with localStorage persistence

- New storage module (packages/game-engine/src/storage.ts) with:
  - GameLog: records every completed game with players, scores, duration, winner
  - HighScores: top 10 per dice count with original/current rank tracking
  - Pure utility functions: addGameLogEntry, updateHighScores, getPlayerAverageScore
- 12 new tests for storage module (all passing)
- Web + Desktop: persist to localStorage, show high score table + avg on finished screen
- Mobile: persist to AsyncStorage, same UI treatment
- handleGameFinished callback with dedup ref (no stale closure issues)
- Player average score shown next to final scores (computed from game log)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: Support dashboard resources auto-downloading (issue #985) (#1062)

* Initial plan

* feat: add dashboard auto-download support (external-ui-url config)

Co-authored-by: ibigbug <543405+ibigbug@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: IPv6 literals fail in direct outbound when dns.ipv6 is disabled (#1063)

* fix(tun): use async sleep to wait for TUN interface visibility before route setup (#1060)

* Initial plan

* fix(tun): wait for tun visibility using async sleep before adding routes

Fixes #1032 - intermittent TUN startup crash "tun interface not found".

After build_async() creates a new TUN device, the interface may not be
immediately visible via NetworkInterface::show(). This caused maybe_add_routes()
to call get_interface_by_name() -> None -> .expect() -> panic (SIGABRT).

Changes:
- Add Clone derive to TunConfig (config/internal/config.rs)
- Replace .expect("tun interface not found") with proper error return in
  maybe_add_routes (routes/mod.rs)
- Make new_internal an async associated function taking &TunConfig; add
  retry loop (up to 40x50ms = 2s) using tokio::time::sleep (not
  std::thread::sleep which blocks Tokio runtime) to wait for TUN
  interface visibility before calling maybe_add_routes (runner.rs)
- Update run_async to clone cfg and await new_internal inside tokio::spawn

Co-authored-by: ibigbug <543405+ibigbug@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(tun): address review comments - fix poll loop and surface NetworkInterface errors

- Restructure TUN visibility poll loop to avoid sleeping after the
  last attempt, eliminating false-negative window where interface
  becomes visible during the final sleep but still errors
- Capture NetworkInterface::show() errors and include the last error
  message in the Error::Operation return for better diagnostics
- Fix fmt/clippy issues in runner.rs

Co-authored-by: ibigbug <543405+ibigbug@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(tun): fix nightly fmt - adjust line break in format string

Co-authored-by: ibigbug <543405+ibigbug@users.noreply.github.com>
